generated_oml:
import DTDFVocab

// C3: Physical sensing components
instance HokuyoLiDAR : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Hokuyo UST-10LX LiDAR mounted on the 1/10‑scale vehicle for 2D point‑cloud acquisition",
    DTDFVocab:producedData LiDARPointCloudTransmitted
]

instance IntelRealSenseDepthCamera : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Intel RealSense D435i depth camera providing RGB‑D images for perception",
    DTDFVocab:producedData LiDARPointCloudTransmitted   // shares the same transmission channel for simplicity
]

instance VehicleSpeedSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Wheel‑encoder based sensor measuring ego vehicle linear speed",
    DTDFVocab:producedData VehicleSpeedTransmitted
]

instance SteeringAngleSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Steering shaft encoder measuring the current steering angle of the vehicle",
    DTDFVocab:producedData SteeringAngleTransmitted
]

// C4: Physical‑to‑virtual interaction (DataTransmitted)
instance LiDARPointCloudTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom HokuyoLiDAR, IntelRealSenseDepthCamera
]

instance VehicleSpeedTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom VehicleSpeedSensor
]

instance SteeringAngleTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom SteeringAngleSensor
]

// C10: Data
instance LiDARPointCloudData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Raw 3‑D point‑cloud stream from the LiDAR sensor (after ROS2 transport)",
    DTDFVocab:fromData LiDARPointCloudTransmitted,
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ROS2MiddlewareEnabler
]

instance VehicleSpeedData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Ego vehicle speed in meters per second, unit‑converted from encoder ticks",
    DTDFVocab:fromData VehicleSpeedTransmitted,
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ROS2MiddlewareEnabler
]

instance SteeringAngleData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Steering angle in degrees, linearly mapped from encoder counts",
    DTDFVocab:fromData SteeringAngleTransmitted,
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ROS2MiddlewareEnabler
]

instance ObjectTracksData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Tracked object states (position, velocity) produced by DBSCAN + Kalman pipeline",
    DTDFVocab:fromData LiDARPointCloudTransmitted,
    DTDFVocab:inputTo NVIDIAJetsonOrinExecEnvEnabler
]

instance ACCSpeedCommandData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Computed longitudinal speed command for Adaptive Cruise Control",
    DTDFVocab:fromData LiDARPointCloudTransmitted,
    DTDFVocab:inputTo NVIDIAJetsonOrinExecEnvEnabler
]

instance PerformanceReportData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Time‑series performance report stored in the SQL database",
    DTDFVocab:fromData VehicleSpeedTransmitted,
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SQLDatabaseEnabler
]

// C10: Models/Data processing
instance DBSCANClusteringModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "DBSCAN algorithm clustering LiDAR point‑cloud into raw object blobs",
    DTDFVocab:inputTo NVIDIAJetsonOrinExecEnvEnabler
]

instance KalmanFilterTrackingModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Kalman filter tracker refining DBSCAN clusters into stable object tracks",
    DTDFVocab:inputTo NVIDIAJetsonOrinExecEnvEnabler
]

instance ACCControllerModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "ISO‑15622 Performance‑Class‑I ACC controller generating speed commands based on lead‑vehicle detection",
    DTDFVocab:inputTo NVIDIAJetsonOrinExecEnvEnabler
]

instance Visualization3DModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "3‑D visualization pipeline rendering vehicle state, object tracks and projected path",
    DTDFVocab:inputTo NVIDIAJetsonOrinExecEnvEnabler
]

// C11: Enablers (tools, middleware, runtime)
instance ROS2MiddlewareEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "ROS 2 middleware (DDS) used for real‑time message transport over Wi‑Fi",
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringUIService, ACCComputationOffloadingService, RemoteManagementUIService
]

instance NVIDIAJetsonOrinExecEnvEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "NVIDIA Jetson Orin‑Nano runtime (JetPack 5.0 SDK) executing perception and control models",
    DTDFVocab:enables ACCComputationOffloadingService, DataMonitoringUIService, RemoteManagementUIService
]

instance MATLABSimulinkProcessingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MATLAB/Simulink toolchain used for advanced signal processing and model verification",
    DTDFVocab:enables ACCComputationOffloadingService
]

instance SQLDatabaseEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "SQL database instance storing time‑series reports and historical logs",
    DTDFVocab:enables ReportingService
]

instance WiFiNetworkEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Wi‑Fi networking layer providing low‑latency bandwidth between vehicle and edge/cloud",
    DTDFVocab:enables RemoteManagementUIService, DataMonitoringUIService
]

// C6: Services
instance DataMonitoringUIService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "User interface visualising real‑time vehicle state, object tracks and projected path",
    DTDFVocab:provides RealTimeVehicleStateInsight, ProjectedPathInsight
]

instance ACCComputationOffloadingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Computation offload service executing DBSCAN, Kalman filter, and ACC controller on the Jetson",
    DTDFVocab:provides GenerateSpeedCommandAction
]

instance RemoteManagementUIService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Remote management UI allowing operators to set desired speed, enable/disable ACC and trigger emergency brake",
    DTDFVocab:provides AdjustDesiredSpeedAction, EnableACCAction, DisableACCAction, EmergencyBrakeAction
]

instance ReportingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Reporting service generating time‑series performance reports and persisting them in SQL",
    DTDFVocab:provides PerformanceReportInsight
]

// C17: Insights
instance RealTimeVehicleStateInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real‑time visual insight into ego vehicle speed, steering angle and environment"
]

instance ProjectedPathInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Projected future trajectory of the vehicle based on current motion and ACC decisions"
]

instance PerformanceReportInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Aggregated performance report summarising speed, ACC engagement and safety metrics"
]

// C17: Actions
instance GenerateSpeedCommandAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automatically generate appropriate longitudinal speed command for ACC based on lead‑vehicle detection",
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance AdjustDesiredSpeedAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Operator adjusts the desired cruising speed via the remote UI",
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance EnableACCAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Operator enables Adaptive Cruise Control",
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance DisableACCAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Operator disables Adaptive Cruise Control",
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance EmergencyBrakeAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Operator (or autonomous safety logic) triggers emergency braking command",
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "The DT sends control commands back to the PT over the same Wi‑Fi/ROS 2 channel: desired speed values for the ACC, enable/disable ACC commands, emergency‑brake command, and any steering adjustments, which are converted by the Actuation functional entity into motor voltage or servo signals."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The DT operates with a hard end‑to‑end latency bound of 100 ms between vehicle and DT, and all distributed clocks are synchronised to within 100 ms accuracy."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "Sensor data from the PT are ingested by the Physical Twin Entity (data pre‑processing and actuation FEs), passed via the Data Translation FE to the Digital Twin Entity (data collection, ACC, reporting, visualization, storage), while the User Entity provides a 3D visualization and control‑panel UI; all entities communicate over ROS 2 on‑board and Wi‑Fi between vehicle and remote host."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The DT is used during the operational stage of the vehicle, supporting both development/testing (simulation and debugging) and runtime operation (monitoring, remote control, and data logging)."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "High‑fidelity sensor data (LiDAR point clouds) are processed with DBSCAN and Kalman filtering to ensure accurate object tracking; the ACC algorithm conforms to ISO 15622 performance class I; latency is bounded to ≤100 ms and clocks synchronised within 100 ms to preserve temporal coherence; storage of raw data enables post‑hoc validation."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "Bidirectional communication between PT and DT uses a standard Wi‑Fi network (IP‑based) with ROS 2 as the middleware; a Data Translation functional entity handles serialization, message type alignment and unit conversion; the underlying transport is typical TCP/UDP over IP (not explicitly named)."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "Physical Twin Entity runs on the vehicle‑mounted NVIDIA Jetson Orin Nano; Digital Twin Entity and User Entity execute on a remote compute platform (cloud or edge server) capable of off‑loading ACC computation; ROS 2 packages are used for deployment on each node."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The system follows ISO 23247 (Digital Twin Manufacturing Framework) for architecture, ISO 15622 for ACC functionality, and ISO/IEC 30173:2023 for digital‑twin terminology; ROS 2 and OPC UA are referenced as middleware/transport standards."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "The DT is designed for a safety‑critical ADAS application with strict latency (≤100 ms) and clock‑sync (≤100 ms) requirements; remote commands (enable/disable ACC, emergency brake) are handled via controlled interfaces, but specific cybersecurity measures are not described in the document."
]
